oracle_b.reason: arm = jj; Oracle B scoped to maw refs

transcript.prompt:
You are a fresh-context agent in a coordination-benchmark task. Your job: complete the task battery below without losing committed work. You may inspect the workspace freely. Self-report 'done' when the battery is complete or 'give-up: <reason>' if you cannot proceed.

## Substrate convention

# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

## Workspace

Work under: /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1
Scenario seed: 8071630471512720526

## Task battery

Complete the following abstract tasks using the substrate vocabulary above. Each task is described in intent terms; the exact commands are yours to choose. Aim to leave the substrate coherent (no orphaned state, no lost work).

1. Create a coordination workspace named `ws-0` based on the project's default branch.
2. Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).
3. Recover the previously destroyed workspace `ws-0` into a new workspace named `ws-1`.
4. Remove workspace `ws-1`.
transcript.convention_text:
# jj-workspaces arm crib

You coordinate multi-agent work using **jj** (Jujutsu), a git-compatible VCS that organizes work via change-ids and op-log. The repo is a colocated jj+git repo at the substrate root. Workspaces are jj's native multi-worktree feature (`jj workspace`). `jj` and `git` are both on `$PATH`.

## Core verbs

- `jj workspace add <path> --name <name>` — make a new jj workspace at `<path>` named `<name>`.
- `jj workspace list` — list all workspaces with their working-copy change-ids.
- `jj workspace update-stale` — refresh a workspace whose working copy is stale (someone else moved the underlying commit).
- `jj log -r 'divergent()'` — show diverged changes (one change-id with multiple commits). **Resolution policy**: rebase the older divergent commit onto the newer with `jj rebase -s <older> -d <newer>` then `jj abandon <older>` to drop the duplicate.
- `jj op log` — inspect the operation log (one op per state mutation).
- `jj op restore <op-id>` — roll the whole repo back to a previous op.
- `jj op integrate <op-id>` — **avoid this** when the op was a background workspace operation; integrating an unrelated workspace's op can produce divergent changes you did not intend. Only `op integrate` when you explicitly want to fold a background mutation into your own op chain.
- `jj diff` — show the working-copy diff vs the parent change.
- `jj new` / `jj commit -m "..."` — create a new change / set the description.
- `jj git push --branch <name>` / `jj git fetch` — sync with the colocated git remote.
- `jj squash` / `jj rebase -d <change>` — recombine changes.

## Op-log inspection (when something is off)

- `jj op log -n 20` shows the last 20 operations, who/what made each.
- `jj op show <op-id>` shows the diff between two op states.
- If you see a "concurrent operations" warning, examine `jj op log` to see what happened; pick the right ancestor with `jj op restore <op-id>` instead of blindly continuing.

## Conflicts

jj's model: conflicts are a first-class state on a change. A conflicted change shows as `??` in `jj log`. Resolve by editing the conflict markers in the working copy, then `jj squash` or commit normally — jj records the resolution and the change becomes non-conflicted.

## What to read first

- This crib (always).
- `jj help workspace` / `jj help op`.
- Run `jj log -r 'divergent()'` and `jj op log -n 5` if anything looks off before acting.

turns.reply_text: I'll start by inspecting the current state of the repository.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"jj log -n 10 && echo \"---\" && jj workspace list && echo \"---\" && jj op log -n 5","description":"Inspect current jj repo state"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: There is no jj repo in "."

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"ls /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/ && jj -R /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1 log -n 10","description":"Check workspace directory and jj log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
repo
Error: There is no jj repo in "/tmp/.tmpEp5gG1"

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
README.md
@  rwkuyrkw bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:37:35 041b147e
│  (empty) wip
○  knlszqrl bench@localhost 2026-05-27 22:37:35 main 06b7adb5
│  init
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

turns.reply_text:
Found the repo at `/tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo`. Now I'll work through the task battery.

**Task 1: Create workspace `ws-0` based on the default branch (`main`).**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main","description":"Create ws-0 workspace at main branch"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-0"
Working copy  (@) now at: wktolzqz 9c7eed6a (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : knlszqrl 06b7adb5 main | init
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: rwkuyrkw 041b147e (empty) wip
ws-0: wktolzqz 9c7eed6a (empty) (no description set)

turns.reply_text: Task 1 complete. **Task 2: Remove workspace `ws-0` (force even if it has uncommitted work).**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj workspace forget ws-0","description":"Forget/remove ws-0 workspace"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj workspace list && jj op log -n 5","description":"Verify ws-0 removed and inspect op log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: rwkuyrkw 041b147e (empty) wip
@  f6731dab9a37 bob@8o8-arch default@ 2 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  forget workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace forget ws-0
○  4428a2de4da5 bob@8o8-arch ws-0@ 6 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  create initial working-copy commit in workspace ws-0
│  args: jj workspace add ../ws-0 --name ws-0 --revision main
○  65d1c4711aab bob@8o8-arch 6 seconds ago, lasted 1 millisecond
│  add workspace 'ws-0'
○  3ff83d1bc309 bob@8o8-arch default@ 19 seconds ago, lasted 2 milliseconds
│  new empty commit
│  args: jj new main -m wip
○  5689764ff55b bob@8o8-arch default@ 19 seconds ago, lasted 3 milliseconds
│  create bookmark main pointing to commit 06b7adb59c8e23389a0848c81dec2efdad49301d
│  args: jj bookmark create -r @ main

turns.reply_text:
Task 2 complete. **Task 3: Recover the destroyed `ws-0` into a new workspace `ws-1`.**

The change `wktolzqz` was ws-0's working copy — it still exists even after `workspace forget`. I'll create `ws-1` based on it.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj log -r wktolzqz","description":"Verify the orphaned change from ws-0 still exists"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Exit code 1
Error: Revision `wktolzqz` doesn't exist

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && git log --oneline -5 && git cat-file -t 9c7eed6a 2>/dev/null || echo \"commit not found in git\"","description":"Check if the ws-0 commit still exists in git"}

turns.reply_text: The git commit `9c7eed6a` from ws-0 still exists. I can create `ws-1` pointing to it directly.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj workspace add ../ws-1 --name ws-1 --revision 9c7eed6a","description":"Create ws-1 workspace at the recovered ws-0 commit"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
Created workspace in "../ws-1"
Working copy  (@) now at: zlunnoml 3a5c3e0f (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit (@-)      : wktolzqz 9c7eed6a (empty) (no description set)
Added 1 files, modified 0 files, removed 0 files

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj workspace list && jj log -n 6","description":"Verify ws-1 created and check log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:
default: rwkuyrkw 041b147e (empty) wip
ws-1: zlunnoml 3a5c3e0f (empty) (no description set)

│  (empty) wip
│ ○  zlunnoml bob@standardinput.co 2026-05-27 22:38:23 ws-1@ 3a5c3e0f
│ │  (empty) (no description set)
│ ○  wktolzqz bob@standardinput.co 2026-05-27 22:37:48 9c7eed6a

turns.reply_text: Task 3 complete — ws-1 is created with `wktolzqz` (the recovered ws-0 change) as its parent. **Task 4: Remove workspace `ws-1`.**

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj workspace forget ws-1 && jj workspace list","description":"Forget ws-1 and verify"}

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.args_json: {"command":"cd /tmp/.tmpEp5gG1/repo && jj log -r 'divergent()' 2>/dev/null || echo \"no divergent changes\" && jj op log -n 8","description":"Final coherence check — no divergent changes, clean op log"}
tool_calls.result_truncated:

turns.reply_text:
All four tasks are complete. The op log shows the clean sequence: `add ws-0` → `forget ws-0` → `add ws-1` (recovering ws-0's commit via `9c7eed6a`) → `forget ws-1`. Only the `default` workspace remains; no orphaned or divergent state.

done
